title: onChange client script to make Assignment Group mandatory for P1 incidents
question: Write an onChange client script that makes Assignment Group mandatory when Priority becomes 1 and optional otherwise.
summary: Client-side form control using g_form in an onChange script.
exactAnswer: Use an onChange client script on the priority field and call g_form.setMandatory('assignment_group', true) when the new value is 1, otherwise set it to false.
steps: Open the incident form client scripts.
Create an onChange client script on the priority field.
Test by changing priority from a non-P1 value to 1 and back.
commonMistakes: Using the field label instead of the field name assignment_group.
Forgetting the isLoading check, which can cause unnecessary form behavior on load.
explanation: The script reacts only to priority changes.
Using == is acceptable here because client field values arrive as strings.

title: UI Action to reopen a closed incident
question: Write a server-side UI Action that reopens a closed incident and resets work notes.
exactAnswer: Create a form UI Action on incident with a condition that shows it only for closed records, then set the state back to In Progress and update work notes before saving.
steps: Create a new form button UI Action on incident.
Set a condition so it appears only when state is Closed.
Paste the server-side script and save.
commonMistakes: Showing the button for all states instead of only closed records.
Forgetting action.setRedirectURL(current), which can lead to awkward navigation after update.
explanation: UI Actions can run server-side and redirect the user after updating the record.

title: Advanced reference qualifier for Assignment Group
question: Write a script-based reference qualifier that shows only active assignment groups.
exactAnswer: Use an advanced reference qualifier that returns an encoded query filtering to active groups.
steps: Open the reference field dictionary or variable configuration.
Test the lookup on the form.
commonMistakes: Overengineering the qualifier when a simple encoded query is enough.
Returning malformed strings from the qualifier script.
explanation: Advanced qualifiers can return an encoded query string directly.

title: Catalog client script to show a field based on request type
question: Write a catalog client script that shows manager_approval_reason only when request_type is Access.
exactAnswer: Use an onChange catalog client script on request_type and call g_form.setDisplay on the variable name based on the selected value.
steps: Create a Catalog Client Script on the request_type variable.
Paste the script and test the form.
commonMistakes: Using the variable question label instead of the variable name.
Forgetting catalog scripts still use g_form for variable behavior.
explanation: Catalog client scripts behave similarly to form client scripts but target variables.
